ddls:
CREATE TABLE "problems" (
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each problem */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the problem */,
  "difficulty" TEXT /* Difficulty level of the problem (e.g., Easy, Medium, Hard) */,
  "acceptance_rate" REAL /* Acceptance rate of the problem */,
  "performance_metric" REAL /* Performance metric for the problem */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL to the problem on LeetCode */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the problem was added to the platform */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the problem details were last updated */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes received by the problem */,
  "dislikes" INTEGER /* Number of dislikes received by the problem */,
  "submission_count" INTEGER /* Total number of submissions for the problem */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("problem_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "problem_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag (e.g., Array, String, Dynamic Programming) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "problem_tag_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* ID of the problem */,
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_problem_tag_mapping_problem_id FOREIGN KEY ("problem_id") REFERENCES problems ("problem_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_problem_tag_mapping_tag_id FOREIGN KEY ("tag_id") REFERENCES problem_tags ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, recruiter, admin) */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the platform */,
  "last_active" TEXT /* Date when the user was last active */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the user belongs to (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "problem_attempts" (
  "attempt_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attempt */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the attempt */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* ID of the problem being attempted */,
  "attempts_made" INTEGER /* Number of attempts made by the user */,
  "time_taken" REAL /* Time taken to solve the problem in minutes */,
  "solved" INTEGER /* Whether the problem was solved (true/false) */,
  "date_attempts" TEXT /* Date of the attempts */,
  "language_used" TEXT /* Programming language used to solve the problem */,
  "code_length" INTEGER /* Length of the code submitted (in characters) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attempt_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_problem_attempts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_problem_attempts_problem_id FOREIGN KEY ("problem_id") REFERENCES problems ("problem_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "solutions" (
  "solution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each solution */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* ID of the problem */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the solution */,
  "solution_text" TEXT /* Text of the solution */,
  "explanation_text" TEXT /* Explanation of the solution */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the solution was last updated */,
  "upvotes" INTEGER /* Number of upvotes the solution has received */,
  "downvotes" INTEGER /* Number of downvotes the solution has received */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("solution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_solutions_problem_id FOREIGN KEY ("problem_id") REFERENCES problems ("problem_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_solutions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the problem */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* ID of the problem accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, attempt, solve) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_problem_id FOREIGN KEY ("problem_id") REFERENCES problems ("problem_id")
)
